TEMPORARY EXEMPTION: Ordinary passport holders may enter visa-free for tourism only, from 15 April 2026 until 31 December 2026, with a valid passport. Outside this window, Saudi ordinary passport holders require a visa (diplomatic/service/official passport holders are separately exempt year-round under a bilateral agreement). Confirmed on official MFA site.

TEMPORARY EXEMPTION: Ordinary passport holders may enter visa-free for tourism only, from 15 April 2026 until 31 December 2026, with a valid passport. Outside this window, Qatar ordinary passport holders require a visa. Confirmed on official MFA site.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not included in visa-free list
e_visa.notes: Foreign citizens from countries not in the visa-free list may apply online at e-visa.al for a Type C or Type D visa. Processing takes up to 15 working days; in exceptional cases up to 30 working days. The e-visa is sent by email once approved.

visa_policy.notes: Visa policy governed by Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners' and Decision of Council of Ministers No. 858, dated 29.12.2021. Albania's visa-free list largely mirrors Schengen Annex II but with notable additions (China, Turkey, Azerbaijan, etc.) and some exclusions. The official MFA website references a detailed country-by-country visa regime table via an interactive dropdown; exact per-country max_stay_days from the official table could not be extracted from the dynamic page. United States citizens receive a special 1-year visa-free period (confirmed by US Embassy Tirana circular, March 8, 2022). Saudi Arabia and Qatar citizens receive a temporary exemption for tourism only (15 April – 31 December 2026) as confirmed on the official MFA site. Seychelles visa-free status sourced from Wikipedia (citing Law No. 79/2021) — not independently verified from official MFA dynamic table. Bahrain, Oman, Thailand, and Indonesia had temporary ordinary-passport exemptions in prior years (2023–2025) but those exemptions did NOT extend into the 2026 season per the official MFA page (only Saudi Arabia and Qatar carry a 2026 temporary exemption).

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa, previously used in a Schengen state

conditional_access.conditions: Visa must be valid, multiple-entry type, and must have been previously used in one of the Schengen states. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the visa.

credential.subtype: valid permit of stay in one of the Schengen states

conditional_access.conditions: Permit must be valid. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the permit.

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa, previously used in the issuing EU member state

conditional_access.conditions: Visa must be valid, multiple-entry type, and must have been previously used in the country of issuance (EU member state, including Cyprus and Ireland). Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the visa.

credential.subtype: valid residence permit issued by an EU member state

conditional_access.conditions: Permit must be valid. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the permit.

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa, previously used in the United States

conditional_access.conditions: Visa must be valid, multiple-entry type, and must have been previously used in the United States. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the visa.
Permit/Green Card must be valid. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the document.

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa, previously used in the United Kingdom

conditional_access.conditions: Visa must be valid, multiple-entry type, and must have been previously used in the United Kingdom. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the visa.

credential.subtype: valid residence permit issued by the United Kingdom (including ILR / settlement)

conditional_access.conditions: Permit must be valid. Traveller must leave Albania no later than 3 days from the date of expiry of the permit.
Permit must be a 10-year residence permit issued by the competent authority of the United Arab Emirates, valid for not less than 1 year from the moment of entry into Albania.

credential.subtype: refugee travel document (Geneva Convention 28 July 1951) or stateless person travel document (NY Convention 28 September 1954) or subsidiary protection travel document, issued by an EU member state

conditional_access.conditions: Document must be valid and issued by an EU member state (alien travel document, refugee travel document, stateless person document, or subsidiary protection document).
Visitors of Albanian ethnicity do not require a visa to enter Albania for a maximum stay of 90 days within any 180-day period.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Algeria may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Brazil may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders have unconditional visa-free access.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Cuba may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Ecuador may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Egypt may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of India may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require an e-visa. No OCI card exemption identified.

Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Indonesia may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Note: Indonesia ordinary passport holders had a temporary exemption in 2025 that was NOT extended into 2026.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Jordan may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Kyrgyzstan may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Mongolia may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Morocco may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of the Philippines may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Russia may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Saudi Arabia may enter Albania visa-free year-round under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders additionally benefit from a temporary tourism visa-free regime (15 April – 31 December 2026) captured in visa_policy.visa_free.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of South Africa may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Thailand may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Note: Thailand ordinary passport holders had a temporary exemption in prior years that was NOT extended into the 2026 season.
Diplomatic passport holders of Tunisia only may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Service/official and ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Uzbekistan may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic, service and official passport holders of Vietnam may enter Albania visa-free under a bilateral agreement. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

cbi.notes: Albania announced plans for a citizenship-by-investment program but suspended it in March 2023 under pressure from the European Commission, which stated such programs are incompatible with EU accession. No official CBI program is currently active. This is confirmed by absence of any official government page for a CBI program and news reports citing PM Edi Rama's suspension announcement.

programs.name: Unique Permit for Investors (Leje Unike per Investitorin)
programs.notes: Based on Article 69 of Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners'. Minimum investment of 10,000,000 ALL (approximately EUR 85,000-90,000) — figure set by joint instruction of the Ministers of Finance and Interior; not independently verifiable from the official law text. One secondary legal source (imidaily.com, 2026) references a possible Law 43/2025 that may have updated investment thresholds significantly (e.g., EUR 300,000 for real estate, EUR 100,000 for business), but this could not be confirmed from any official Albanian government publication. Requires employment of 5 Albanian citizens per foreign investor. Initial permit valid 2 years, renewable for 3 years. After 5 years of legal residence, eligible for permanent residency. Standard naturalization requires 7 years of continuous residence.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate Ownership
programs.notes: Based on Article 84 of Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners'. A residence permit can be obtained through registered real estate ownership in Albania (applicant must own at least 50%). No statutory minimum investment amount is defined in the law. After 5 years of continuous legal residence, eligible for permanent residency permit. Standard naturalization requires 7 years of continuous residence.

programs.name: Unique Permit for Digital Mobile Workers (Nomad/Remote Work)
programs.notes: Introduced under Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners', for remote workers employed by or freelancing for companies/clients outside Albania. Available via e-Albania portal (service code 15146). Structure: 1 year initial + 1 year renewal + up to 5-year permit from year 3. Minimum income threshold referenced in secondary sources (approx. EUR 450/month); not independently verified from official law text. After 5 years of continuous legal residence, eligible for permanent residency.

programs.name: Unique Permit for Digital Mobile Workers
programs.eligibility: Remote workers employed by or freelancing for companies/clients established outside Albania. Must have valid employment or service contract with a foreign entity and sufficient income to cover accommodation and living expenses in Albania.
programs.processing_time: Up to 12 weeks (maximum) from date of application
programs.notes: Available via the e-Albania portal (service code 15146). Application submitted entirely online. Introduced in 2022 under Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners'. Combines residence and work authorization in a single biometric card.

programs.name: Unique Permit for Scientific Research
programs.eligibility: Foreign researchers undertaking scientific research at Albanian research institutions or universities. Based on Article 50 of Law No. 79/2021.
programs.notes: Based on Article 50 of Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners'. Available via e-Albania portal.

programs.name: Job Seeking / Entrepreneurship Permit for Researchers and Graduates
programs.eligibility: Researchers and highly qualified graduates seeking employment or establishing a business in Albania. Based on Article 51 of Law No. 79/2021.
programs.notes: Based on Article 51 of Law No. 79/2021 'On Foreigners'. Provides a pathway for graduates and researchers to seek employment or start a business in Albania.

outbound_official.notes: Albania's Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs (punetejashtme.gov.al) publishes an interactive country-lookup tool at /en/regjimi-i-vizave-per-shtetasit-shqiptare-info/ where Albanian ordinary-passport citizens can select a destination country and see the applicable visa regime. The landing/intro page is at /en/regjimi-i-vizave-per-shtetasit-shqiptare-info-per-qytetaret-shqiptare/ and states the information is 'indicative, according to official announcements of foreign countries forwarded to the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs'. A separate official sub-page at /en/levizja-e-lire-ne-vendet-e-hapesires-shengen/ covers Schengen area access (90 days within 6 months, tourism/business only, biometric passport required). The dropdown tool covers 195+ destination countries. Full per-country visa categorisation could not be statically extracted due to JavaScript rendering. Schengen visa-free list (29 countries) extracted from the dedicated Schengen sub-page. No date stamp shown on the official pages.

agreements: Stabilisation and Association Agreement with EU (in force since April 2009)
Visa Liberalisation with Schengen Area (since December 2010 — Albanian citizens travel visa-free to Schengen for short stays)
SEPA (Single Euro Payments Area, joined November 2024)

validation_notes: ADDED Seychelles (SYC) to visa_free list (90 days, ordinary passport). Found in Wikipedia citing Law No. 79/2021 but was absent from prior record. Source_official set to false as it could not be extracted from the official MFA dynamic table directly.

CLARIFIED Saudi Arabia and Qatar 2026 temporary exemption notes: these are strictly tourism-only and limited to 15 April – 31 December 2026. Saudi Arabia correctly appears in both visa_free (temporary, ordinary passport) and conditional_access (year-round, diplomatic/service/official passport).
CONFIRMED US 1-year visa-free is correct: corroborated by US Embassy Tirana circular (March 8, 2022). Updated USA entry notes to cite this source.

CONFIRMED Bahrain, Oman, Thailand, Indonesia do NOT have ordinary-passport visa-free access in 2026: the official MFA page confirms only Saudi Arabia and Qatar carry a 2026 temporary exemption. Bahrain, Oman, Thailand, Indonesia had prior-year temporary exemptions that ended with the 2025 season. Indonesia and Thailand appear only in conditional_access (diplomatic/service/official passports).
ADDED clarifying note to Indonesia and Thailand conditional_access entries explaining that ordinary passport temporary exemptions from prior years were NOT extended into 2026.

RETAINED all other entries unchanged — all credential-based conditional_access rules (Schengen, EU, US, UK, UAE Golden Visa) and special_status (Albanian ethnicity) entries remain as previously verified.

RBI investor permit min_amount of 10,000,000 ALL retained: secondary source referenced a possible Law 43/2025 that may have updated thresholds but this could not be confirmed from any official Albanian government publication. Noted in gaps.
gaps: The official MFA visa regime table at punetejashtme.gov.al is rendered as an interactive JavaScript dropdown; the full per-country list with exact max_stay_days could not be programmatically extracted. Country names in the visa_free list are cross-referenced from the Wikipedia article on Visa policy of Albania, which cites Law No. 79/2021 and Council of Ministers Decision No. 858/2021 as its basis.

Seychelles visa-free status sourced only from Wikipedia (citing Law No. 79/2021), not independently confirmed from the official MFA dynamic dropdown table. Source_official set to false for this entry.

The exact minimum investment amount for the Investor Unique Permit (Article 69) is set by joint ministerial instruction (Ministers of Finance and Interior) rather than in the primary law text. One secondary source referenced a possible Law 43/2025 that may have substantially revised thresholds (e.g., EUR 300,000 for real estate, EUR 100,000 for business), but this law could not be confirmed from any official Albanian government publication as of June 2026.
Albania's CBI program was announced but suspended in March 2023; no official government page exists to confirm current status. Absence of a program is inferred from lack of any active official page.
Minimum income requirement for the Digital Nomad Unique Permit (cited as EUR 450/month) was found only in secondary sources, not on the official e-albania.al page.

The UAE 10-year residence permit (Golden Visa) as a conditional access basis was confirmed in prior record based on the official MFA page, but could not be re-extracted from the live MFA page in this validation cycle due to dynamic rendering. Retained on prior-cycle authority.

Exact list of countries eligible for diplomatic passport visa exemptions cannot be independently confirmed from the official dynamic MFA table. The list is sourced from Wikipedia (Visa policy of Albania), which cites the official Albanian MFA page.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card exemption was found for Albania in any official source.
The diplomatic/service passport waiver list does not include specific bilateral agreement dates or names for most countries.

Status of Bahrain and Oman 2026 exemptions: the official MFA page confirms only Saudi Arabia and Qatar for 2026. Bahrain and Oman ordinary-passport exemptions observed in 2023 and possibly 2024/2025 appear to have lapsed. Their diplomatic passport status (if any) was not independently confirmed — they do not appear in the current record's conditional_access diplomatic list and that omission is retained.
No confirmation found that Thailand ordinary-passport exemption extended into 2026 — the official MFA page names only Saudi Arabia and Qatar for 2026.
